parentQuestion: A patient presents with numbness and tingling in a 'glove-and-stocking' distribution — affecting hands and feet but sparing proximal limbs and trunk. What pattern of nerve damage does this distribution suggest?

scaffolds.q: The nerves that go to your toes are longer than the nerves that go to your knee.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Nerves to the toes travel from the spinal cord all the way to the feet - about 1 meter. Nerves to the knee are much shorter.

scaffolds.q: Longer axons need more nutrients and have higher metabolic demands.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Everything needed by the axon terminal must travel from the cell body. Longer distance = more transport needed = more vulnerable.

scaffolds.q: When nerves are stressed by disease (diabetes, alcohol, toxins), the longest nerves fail first.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! This is called length-dependent neuropathy. The longest supply lines fail before shorter ones.

scaffolds.q: 'Glove-and-stocking' refers to numbness in the hands (like gloves) and feet (like stockings).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The pattern looks like wearing gloves and stockings. The numbness is in the most distal (distant) parts of the limbs.

scaffolds.q: In glove-and-stocking pattern, the upper arms and thighs are SPARED (normal).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Only the hands and feet (served by the longest nerves) are affected. The proximal limbs (shorter nerves) are fine.

scaffolds.q: This pattern is SYMMETRIC - both hands and both feet are affected equally.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Both left and right sides are affected equally because it's a metabolic/toxic process, not a localized injury.

scaffolds.q: Common causes of glove-and-stocking neuropathy include diabetes, alcoholism, and chemotherapy.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! These conditions damage nerves through metabolic stress. The longest nerves (to hands and feet) die first.

scaffolds.q: The pattern of symptoms (distal, symmetric) tells you the TYPE of nerve damage.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Geography is diagnosis. Glove-and-stocking = length-dependent. One-sided = stroke or local injury. Pattern reveals cause.
Length-dependent peripheral neuropathy! The longest nerves fail first, creating the symmetric distal pattern. Diabetes, alcohol, and toxins cause this.
